title: Operator TUI: Carbonyl WebRTC/DataChannel session with OIDC and modern browser capabilities
summary: Add an explicit WebRTC-capable Carbonyl center-browser session for Ananta. Core realtime skeletons exist, but the full RTCPeerConnection/DataChannel runtime, TUI command wiring and test evidence are not complete yet.

progress_assessment.reason: Option C, signaling models/client, DataChannel protocol, policy and docs exist. Actual complete PeerConnection/DataChannel runtime and tests were not sufficiently verified. Some expected tests are not currently fetchable by path.

design_principle: WebRTC is not just rendering. It is a network/session/runtime feature. Carbonyl may host the browser page in the CenterViewport, but Ananta must own policy, identity binding, signaling configuration, permissions and audit state.

accepted_architecture_decision.decision: Option C - keep existing Hub relay and build a separate realtime WebRTC stack
accepted_architecture_decision.meaning: client_surfaces/operator_tui/webrtc_transport.py remains the existing Share-Session Hub relay path and must not be treated as real WebRTC. The new client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/ stack is the real Carbonyl/WebRTC path using WebSocket signaling plus RTCDataChannel. The two paths stay separate and are only joined at a higher session/controller level when explicitly needed.
accepted_architecture_decision.renaming_guidance: webrtc_transport.py should be renamed or wrapped as hub_relay_transport.py in a later compatibility task, because the existing implementation uses HTTP polling against the Ananta Hub and not RTCDataChannel. Existing imports may keep a deprecated compatibility alias until callers are migrated.
required_separation: Hub relay path: HTTP polling, Ananta Hub auth, existing Share-Session/TUI operator relay use case.
Realtime path: WebSocket signaling, OIDC-derived session nonce, allowlisted signaling server, RTCPeerConnection and RTCDataChannel.
No raw provider tokens are sent through signaling or DataChannel.
No browser/WebRTC feature depends on the legacy Hub relay transport.

task_state_contract.rule: Every implementation task has its own status. When a task is implemented and verified, change that task status to done and add done_at plus evidence. Do not mark the whole TODO done until all implementation_tasks are done.

known_gaps: WebRtcSessionController currently looks like a signaling/session state orchestrator, not a full RTCPeerConnection/RTCDataChannel implementation.
Expected tests under tests/client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/ were not fetchable by direct path during review.
OIDC-specific BrowserModeController wiring is currently blocked in the OIDC TODO and therefore WebRTC startup from authenticated Carbonyl session is not fully closed.
TUI commands and full E2E smoke output still need concrete verification evidence.
non_goals: Do not enable camera or microphone by default.
Do not assume WSL2 terminal device access works for media.
Do not use WebRTC as a replacement for OIDC identity proof.
Do not allow arbitrary remote signaling servers without allowlist.
Do not persist received artifacts without explicit policy decision.
Do not retrofit real WebRTC into the existing Hub relay transport.

components.name: CarbonylBrowserModeController
components.responsibility: Launches Carbonyl in the CenterViewport and loads the local WebRTC app or authenticated remote app.

components.name: OidcAuthController
components.responsibility: Provides login/session identity before WebRTC session start.

components.name: WebRtcSessionController
components.responsibility: Owns realtime signaling state, peer identity binding, channel lifecycle and policy decisions. Current implementation still needs full RTCPeerConnection/DataChannel runtime verification.

components.name: SignalingClient
components.responsibility: Connects to configured allowlisted WebSocket signaling endpoint such as wss://webrtc.ananta.de/signaling.

components.name: DataChannelProtocol
components.responsibility: Defines structured RTCDataChannel messages for ping/pong, control events, artifact metadata and chunk transfer.

components.name: PermissionPolicy
components.responsibility: Controls datachannel, remote URL, camera, microphone, file/artifact exchange and TURN usage.

components.name: HubRelayTransport
components.responsibility: Compatibility name for the existing HTTP polling Share-Session relay. This is separate from the realtime WebRTC path.

evidence: docs/operator-tui/carbonyl-webrtc-session.md contains Option C section and table separating webrtc_transport.py Hub Relay from realtime/ WebRTC DataChannel.
client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/signaling_client.py module docstring explicitly states Option C separation from webrtc_transport.py.
client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/signaling_models.py states it is completely separate from webrtc_transport.py.
implementation_tasks.title: Document and enforce Option C transport split

evidence: Commit 3211b92606c28be382eb72f6e642f1276070fa6f claims visual/browser/webrtc_app/ index.html, app.js, datachannel.js, style.css, media_probe.js.
remaining_work: Verify the files exist and are actually loaded by Carbonyl browser mode.
Verify app performs no remote fetch before explicit WebRTC start.
implementation_tasks.title: Create local WebRTC browser app loaded by Carbonyl

evidence: client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/signaling_client.py exists and implements allowlist check before connect().
client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/signaling_models.py exists with join/leave/offer/answer/ice_candidate/error/heartbeat envelope and session_nonce field.
No direct dependency on webrtc_transport.py observed in reviewed realtime signaling files.
SignalingClient now parses URLs and requires scheme, host, port and configured endpoint path to match; prefix sibling hosts are rejected.
commands.py no longer auto-adds an arbitrary configured signaling_url to allowed_servers.
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime -q => 21 passed in 2.85s, including test_signaling_client.py.
implementation_tasks.title: Implement allowlisted WebSocket signaling client

implementation_tasks.blocker: The boundary is documented, but full bridge integration depends on OIDC/browser session wiring that is currently blocked in todo.operator-tui-carbonyl-oidc-session-modes.json.
evidence: webrtc_session_controller.py documents that Python owns signaling/policy and browser JS receives only derived metadata.
No complete runtime bridge from authenticated Carbonyl OIDC session into WebRTC app was verified.
remaining_work: Implement or verify actual window.ANANTA_CONFIG injection/path into the WebRTC app.
Connect OIDC subject hash/session nonce generation to WebRtcSessionController startup.
Verify browser app can establish DataChannel without raw provider tokens.
implementation_tasks.title: Bridge Carbonyl browser app and Ananta runtime safely

evidence: client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/datachannel_protocol.py exists with DataChannelMessage, DataChannelProtocol, version checking, size checks and SHA-256 verification helper.
remaining_work: Verify JS datachannel.js stays in sync with Python protocol.
Add or restore tests/client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/test_datachannel_protocol.py; direct search did not find it.
Record real ping/pong smoke test before marking done.
implementation_tasks.title: Define and implement Ananta DataChannel protocol v1

evidence: client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime/webrtc_policy.py exists with deny-by-default camera/microphone/screen-share and explicit_accept_required artifact policy.
webrtc_session_controller.py stores oidc_subject_hash and session_nonce and exposes status.

WebRtcSessionController now requires OIDC subject hash and session_nonce when policy requires them, rejects session_id/session_nonce mismatches, rejects unknown empty peers and preserves controller session_id in accept/reject messages.
OidcAuthController.build_realtime_identity() provides non-secret subject_hash/session_nonce metadata for realtime handoff.
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime -q => 21 passed in 2.85s, including test_webrtc_policy.py and test_webrtc_session_controller.py.
implementation_tasks.title: Bind WebRTC peer/session to OIDC identity and Ananta policy

evidence: Commit 3211b92606c28be382eb72f6e642f1276070fa6f claims realtime/ice_probe.py and scripts/operator_tui_webrtc_smoke.py.
.venv/bin/python scripts/operator_tui_webrtc_smoke.py --mock --indent 0 => summary mock: all probes succeeded.
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime -q => 21 passed in 2.85s, including test_ice_probe.py for STUN/TURN URL parsing and IP redaction.
implementation_tasks.title: Add ICE/STUN/TURN capability and connectivity probes

evidence: Commit 3211b92606c28be382eb72f6e642f1276070fa6f claims media_probe.js.
WebRtcPolicy defaults camera_enabled, microphone_enabled and screen_share_enabled to false.
remaining_work: Verify media_probe.js exists and is only called by explicit user action.
implementation_tasks.title: Add optional audio/video/camera/microphone capability experiment

implementation_tasks.blocker: WebRTC UI controls depend on completed browser/OIDC session bridge and verified TUI command wiring.
evidence: WebRtcSessionController exposes get_status() with auth/signaling/ice/datachannel/peer/transfer state.
Actual TUI commands center.browser.webrtc.start/stop/status/accept_artifact were not verified in this review.
remaining_work: Wire commands into keybindings/config and runtime view controller.
Verify Esc cleanup from active WebRTC mode.
implementation_tasks.title: Add TUI controls/status for WebRTC mode

evidence: Commit 3211b92606c28be382eb72f6e642f1276070fa6f claims realtime/webrtc_audit.py with redacted audit ring buffer.
WebRtcSessionController.stop_session() disconnects signaling and sets states closed/disconnected.
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/client_surfaces/operator_tui/realtime -q => 21 passed in 2.85s, including test_webrtc_audit.py for token/IP scrubbing and ring-buffer behavior.
Session controller tests cover cleanup-relevant signaling state and session id/nonce rejection paths.
implementation_tasks.title: Audit and cleanup WebRTC sessions safely

evidence: docs/operator-tui/carbonyl-webrtc-session.md exists and explains Option C, security boundary, package layout and limited test services.
Docs clearly mark audio/video experimental and disabled by default.
examples/operator-tui/webrtc/README.md now documents the local mock ICE probe and realtime protocol/policy test command.
docs/operator-tui/carbonyl-oidc-session-modes.md documents OIDC modes, token ownership and smoke commands.
implementation_tasks.title: Document WebRTC + OIDC + Carbonyl setup

mermaid_architecture:
flowchart TD
    A[User starts WebRTC mode in Operator TUI] --> B{OIDC session exists?}
    B -->|no| C[Carbonyl OIDC login flow]
    B -->|yes| D[Load local WebRTC app in Carbonyl]
    C --> D
    D --> E[Connect to allowlisted WebSocket signaling server]
    E --> F[Exchange offer answer ICE]
    F --> G[RTCPeerConnection]
    G --> H[RTCDataChannel]
    H --> I[hello + identity/session nonce handshake]
    I --> J{Policy allows artifact exchange?}
    J -->|yes explicit accept| K[Chunked artifact transfer + SHA256 verify]
    J -->|no| L[Reject with reason]
    K --> M[Audit redacted completion]
    L --> M
    N[Esc/stop] --> O[Close channel peer connection signaling Carbonyl mode]
    P[Existing webrtc_transport.py] --> Q[HubRelayTransport HTTP polling]
    Q --> R[Existing Share-Session relay]
    P -. separate from Option C .- E
acceptance_summary: Option C split is done and documented.
Core realtime skeleton exists but must remain in_progress until RTCPeerConnection/DataChannel runtime and tests are verified.
Signaling and protocol files exist but tests must be added/restored and run.
Browser/OIDC bridge and TUI controls are blocked/in_progress, not done.
No task may be marked done without concrete evidence from code plus tests or smoke output.
